sections.paragraph: (for רְוָד with Aleph prosthetic, probably “a wandering,” “place of fugitives,” from the root רוּד which see), [Arvad], Arad, pr.n. a city of Plaœnicia, on an island of the same name, situated not far from the shore; according to Strabo (xvi. 2, § 13, 14), built by Sidonian fugitives (see the etymology just given). Eze 27:8, 11 Eze 27:11. The Arabian geographers write the name رواد [Ruwâd], and the same name is still used. See Rosenmüller, Alterthumskunde, ii. 1, page 6, seq. The gentile noun is אַרְוָדִי Gen 10:18 1Ch 1:16.
